objects.title: ORION standard lives inside the InfraPilot.v01 repo, not a separate repo
objects.description: ORION_STANDARD.md, RFC/, and Skills/ were merged as top-level sibling directories in github.com/monuzalejo-sys/InfraPilot.v01, alongside the existing InfraPilot product files (blueprint, database-design, ui-ux-design, demo-flow, infrapilot-app).
objects.reason: User explicitly chose this over a separate repo or a separate branch when asked how to integrate ORION with the existing InfraPilot repo.

objects.fact: GitHub pushes from this automated terminal environment cannot complete: the Git Credential Manager auth dialog fails ('User cancelled dialog', no /dev/tty). Pushes must be done through GitHub Desktop (already authenticated on this machine) instead.

objects.fact: A malformed path '.gitignoregit add .' existed in the original InfraPilot.v01 history (trailing-dot filename, invalid on Windows/Win32). It duplicated the content of the real .gitignore and was dropped during the history merge (commit 1589f8b) rather than carried forward.
objects.context: Likely an accidental shell paste from a prior Mac session (repo also contained a stray .DS_Store).

objects.title: infrapilot-app registered as a proper git submodule pointing at monuzalejo-sys/InfraPilot.Ai
objects.description: The infrapilot-app gitlink existed since the first InfraPilot commit but had no .gitmodules entry, making its source unrecoverable from InfraPilot.v01 alone. Confirmed via 'git ls-remote' that the gitlink commit (547af1a) is the exact HEAD of github.com/monuzalejo-sys/InfraPilot.Ai, and wired it up with a real .gitmodules.
objects.reason: Needed to actually clone and inspect the existing app before deciding what to build next.

objects.fact: infrapilot-app is NOT a greenfield project. It's a working Next.js 16.2.7 / React 19.2.4 / Tailwind 4 demo with: dashboard, cotizador (AI quoting via Anthropic + Groq SDKs), APUs, licitaciones, precios, predictor financiero, presupuestos, login/register, Excel export (xlsx), and API routes wired to Supabase (@supabase/ssr). It is already deployed on Vercel and reachable outside this machine (per user, 2026-07-01). Locally verified: 'npm install' + 'npm run dev' start cleanly, all main routes (/, /login, /dashboard, /cotizador, /apus) return HTTP 200.
objects.context: Discovered while analyzing what 'construir el frontend de InfraPilot AI' should mean — completely changed the task from 'build' to 'refine/complete'.

objects.fact: No .env file exists locally for infrapilot-app. UI pages render using lib/mock-*.ts data files (mock-data, mock-colombia-apus, mock-colombia-licitaciones, mock-colombia-materials, mock-colombia-projects, mock-budget-detail, mock-licitacion, mock-predictor). API routes (app/api/budgets, /apus, /prices, /cotizar, /licitaciones/analyze, /projects) are for-real wired to Supabase and require an authenticated user — without env vars configured, proxy.ts lets all traffic through (auth check is skipped) but calls to these routes would fail against a real Supabase client. supabase/migrations only has 001_prices_apus.sql so far.
objects.context: Local Supabase config may or may not match what's set on Vercel — not yet verified whether Vercel's deployment has real working credentials or is also running in a degraded/mock mode.

objects.constraint: infrapilot-app/AGENTS.md (imported by CLAUDE.md) explicitly warns: this Next.js version (16.2.7) has breaking API/convention/file-structure changes vs. what's in an LLM's training data. MUST read the relevant guide in node_modules/next/dist/docs/ before writing any new Next.js code in this app, and heed deprecation notices.
objects.reason: Author of infrapilot-app left this instruction deliberately — ignoring it risks writing code against outdated Next.js APIs.

objects.reason: Phase A DONE 2026-07-02 (verified PASS 5/5): schema.sql moved to migrations/000_initial_schema.sql (001 depends on it), .env.example created (the ONLY 3 vars the code reads), supabase/README.md documents run order + auth model + Vercel setup. NO missing tables (000+001 cover every .from() call); login/register is pure Supabase Auth, no custom profile table. Phase B blocked on user: create/choose Supabase project, provide NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL + NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY (+ optional GROQ_API_KEY), run the 2 migrations in order, set same 3 vars on Vercel. Then verify login/register/persistence live. 2026-07-03: local creds NOW configured — .env.local created with real NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL (https://zrogravgwhoijajzciem.supabase.co) + sb_publishable_ anon key (gitignored, verified not staged). STILL PENDING by user: run the 2 migrations in Supabase SQL editor (000 then 001), set the 3 vars in Vercel env, confirm Vercel-GitHub Git link. Then live-verify real-mode login/register/persistence.

objects.task: Push local commits (d68fac0, f971daa, d0cbb2d, and this session's memory-update commit) to origin/main via GitHub Desktop.

objects.description: The ORION lifecycle (ANALYZING -> PLANNING -> BUILDING -> VERIFYING -> FIXING -> REFLECTING -> DONE/ESCALATED) is invoked as a Claude Code skill at C:\Users\Kalel\.claude\skills\orion\SKILL.md ('/orion <task>'), installed at user level so it works across all projects on this machine, not just InfraPilot. BUILDING delegates substantial independent work to real Agent subagents; REFLECTING writes directly to memory/<projectId>/state.json and metrics.json following the AMM schema (no live TS runtime executes the AMM source — the assistant emulates its contract by hand).
objects.pattern: Manually-operated Cognitive Runtime: the assistant itself plays the runtime role each invocation, rather than a standalone always-on process.

objects.title: Six dedicated ORION subagents created, mapped to the RFC-0002 contracts
objects.description: Created user-level Claude Code subagents in C:\Users\Kalel\.claude\agents\ : orion-analyst (orion:analysis:v1, read-only), orion-planner (orion:planning:v1, read-only), orion-builder (orion:build:v1, full tools, one per plan step, parallel where independent), orion-verifier (orion:verification:v1, read+run-tests only, no edits), orion-fixer (orion:fix:v1, edits only the artifacts a QAReport names), and orion-reflector (orion:reflection:v1 + orion:memory:v1, writes to memory/*.json). Tools are restricted per contract (e.g. analyst/planner/verifier cannot modify files). The /orion SKILL.md was updated so each phase delegates to its named agent; the main conversation acts as the orchestrator/runtime. Prior to this, /orion only used the generic built-in agent types (Explore/general-purpose/Plan) — no custom ORION agents existed. Verified live 2026-07-01: agents load in-session (no restart needed) and orion-analyst returned a correct response.
objects.reason: User asked whether the agents were created correctly; they were not (only the skill existed). User authorized creating them ('tienes acceso total'). Mapping agents 1:1 to the ORION contracts makes the system faithful to the user's own standard rather than generic.

objects.title: ORION model policy: adaptive by difficulty, ceiling Opus
objects.description: The orchestrator picks each agent's model by task/step difficulty via the Agent tool's model param: trivial->haiku, normal->sonnet, hard->opus (ceiling Opus is allowed; floor Haiku). The orion-analyst rates difficulty per sub-objective/step and those ratings drive builder/verifier/fixer model choice; builders run one-per-step so a single plan can mix haiku and opus steps in parallel. Agent frontmatter defaults were set (analyst/planner/builder/verifier/fixer = sonnet, reflector = haiku) so a naive spawn doesn't inherit the main conversation's model. Full rubric lives in the '1b. Model selection' section of ~/.claude/skills/orion/SKILL.md (mirrored in runtime/skills/).
objects.reason: User chose adaptive-by-difficulty with an Opus ceiling. This environment has no live token metering (RFC-0004 budgets are conceptual here), so model choice is the only real cost lever — spending should track difficulty instead of defaulting everything to the most expensive model.

objects.title: ORION autocommits on verification PASS; push stays manual
objects.description: Standing policy: when an ORION task passes VERIFYING, autocommit the changes in whichever repo changed (app code -> infrapilot-app submodule / InfraPilot.Ai; memory & docs -> parent InfraPilot.v01), with a clear message. Do NOT push (terminal push blocked, KN-001) — list pending commits for the user to push via GitHub Desktop. Escalated/failed tasks are not auto-committed.
objects.reason: User chose 'autocommit al pasar verificación' so work is checkpointed automatically without losing the manual-push safety gate.

objects.fact: infrapilot-app's next build was failing because the Groq client was instantiated at module scope in app/api/cotizar/route.ts and app/api/licitaciones/analyze/route.ts, causing the build to fail when GROQ_API_KEY was not in the environment. FIXED by moving client instantiation into the request handlers (after the missing key guard) and committed as 5efdde3. Pattern: never instantiate API-key-dependent clients at module scope in Next route files — construct them inside the handler after validating the key is present.
objects.context: This was F2 in the audit: classified as REAL BUG (build-breaking). Deletion of 6 mock-*.ts files (F5, ~2,650 lines removed) would have failed the build until this was fixed.
objects.source: session 2026-07-01, orion-analyst audit + orion-fixer fix + orion-verifier PASS (commit 5efdde3)

objects.task: F1 (Excel export): lib/excel-export.ts always exports hardcoded mock data (mockGeneratedBudget), ignoring the real AI-generated budget from parameters. Decision needed: keep demo export mode with a flag, or parameterize exportPresupuestoExcel(budget) to export real data.
objects.reason: Escalated finding from orion-analyst audit. Not safe to fix without understanding product intent (demo vs. real export). Marked ESCALATED per contract because behavior change could break user flows if misunderstood.

Fixing all 6 correctly could alter data-loading behavior (render waterfall, stale deps, etc.). Need reviewed batch fix or explicit suppression decision.
objects.reason: Escalated finding from orion-analyst audit. Too risky to auto-fix without domain understanding of the page load flows; each page may have intentional patterns. Needs either explicit suppression via eslint comment or careful UX review.

objects.task: F10-router (unused router vars): login/page.tsx:19 and sidebar.tsx:163 declare unused 'router' variables. Verify they are not intended-but-unwired navigation (e.g. logout redirect) before deleting.
objects.reason: Escalated finding from orion-analyst audit. Low risk but non-obvious whether these are dead code or future-wired; needs user confirmation.

objects.task: Stray lockfile: C:\Users\Kalel\package-lock.json exists outside infrapilot-app and may affect other projects on this machine. Do not delete without explicit user confirmation.
objects.reason: Escalated finding from orion-analyst audit. Not a code quality issue but a housekeeping one; safest approach is to ask user first.

objects.fact: ORION agent hardening for autonomy: all 6 agents (analyst, planner, builder, verifier, fixer, reflector) now enforce single-responsibility boundaries. Builder/fixer cannot commit; analyst/planner/verifier read-only; reflector writes memory only. All agents have hard output caps (e.g., builder max 4000 chars per step, analyst max 3000 per finding). Model policy: haiku for trivial/routine, sonnet for normal, opus for hard tasks (ceiling Opus); planner rates difficulty per step and builder inherits ratings to spawn parallel subagents at appropriate model tier. Reflector baked in AMM schema (no runtime parsing of knowledge-objects.ts); records modelOutcomes per agent spawn (phase, model, verdict) for learning loop.
objects.context: Session 2026-07-02 hardened the ORION ecosystem for continuous autonomy; E2E test passed with zero fix cycles (analyst→planner→builder→verifier→reflector, haiku/sonnet mix).

objects.fact: AMM Schema invariant: tier MUST match lifetime per RFC-0002 §5.2. Tier (Working/Project/Permanent) is a Kubernetes-like cardinality constraint: Working items are Session-scoped; Project items are Sprint/Project lifetime; Permanent items are Permanent lifetime. The validator (tools/validate-memory.mjs) caught real violations in session 2026-07-02: KN-001..005 initially had lifetime=Permanent tier=Project (invalid); repair was KN-001→tier Permanent (machine-level fact justifies permanence); KN-002..005→lifetime Project (facts about infrapilot-app project, not eternal truths). Lesson: future architects must validate tier⟷lifetime pairing on every memory write.
objects.context: Discovered during validation of the E2E pipeline; zero-dep Node.js validator runs offline and caught violations that would have persisted indefinitely if not automated.

objects.title: AMM schema embedded in reflector agent, not runtime-parsed
objects.description: Instead of the reflector loading knowledge-objects.ts at runtime to validate/parse the AMM schema, the schema is baked into the agent's SYSTEM prompt as a table + constraints (LIFETIME_TO_TIER, status values, type-specific fields). This eliminates a runtime TS parser dependency, reduces latency (no file I/O per reflection), and makes the schema version explicit per run (reflector inherits it from its frontmatter at agent load time). If the schema evolves, the reflector's SYSTEM prompt is updated; no AMM-runtime process exists to bump a version field.
objects.reason: Session 2026-07-02 verified that a hand-emulated schema (no live runtime) is viable for ORION's current scale. Baking the schema into the reflector is safer and simpler than attempting to maintain a separate parser binary.

objects.fact: infrapilot-app's tax-by-currency table is hardcoded in lib/utils.ts with standard legal rates: COP and MXN use IVA @19% and @16% respectively; PEN uses IGV @18%; fallback is 'Impuesto' @18%. This branching is correct for the current 3-currency deployment (COP primary), but if InfraPilot expands to additional countries or wants to pull rates from an external source of truth (tax authority APIs, a database, etc.), the helpers (taxLabel(), defaultTaxRate()) must be refactored to consume dynamic configuration instead of literal values.
objects.context: Extracted from PEND-006 F3 (Tax rate mismatch) resolution. The hardcoded rates are intentional and correct per currency, but the pattern (literal switch on currency) is brittle if country coverage expands.

objects.title: Proportional lifecycle: ceremony scales with task size
objects.description: Standing policy (SKILL.md 1d): trivial tasks (1 file, fully specified) run fully inline with zero agent spawns; small tasks (2-4 files) use at most one builder plus a spawned verifier; only substantial multi-step work pays the full analyst->planner->builders->verifier->reflector pipeline. Reflection (memory update) is mandatory at every level, inline or spawned. Rationale: the E2E test showed the full pipeline costs ~10x inline execution for small artifacts (~168k subagent tokens for a 91-line README).
objects.reason: User asked for minimum token consumption per task; full-pipeline-by-default was the single largest waste. Verified 2026-07-02: this session's improvement batch ran inline at a small fraction of the E2E cost.

objects.fact: A pre-commit hook validates ORION memory on every commit of this repo: staged changes to memory/*/state.json or metrics.json trigger tools/validate-memory.mjs and an INVALID result blocks the commit. The versioned copy lives at tools/hooks/pre-commit; the ACTIVE copy at .git/hooks/pre-commit is NOT tracked by git, so after a fresh clone it must be reinstalled with: cp tools/hooks/pre-commit .git/hooks/pre-commit
objects.context: Installed 2026-07-02 so schema enforcement doesn't depend on any agent remembering to run the validator. The validator also gained a --stats flag that prints a model-calibration table from metrics.json modelOutcomes.

objects.task: Optionally make the AMM TypeScript reference implementation real: add package.json + tsconfig + vitest, convert tests/TEST-CASES.md into executable tests, implement the N-AMM-R9 demotion branch, and replace/lower the Jaccard 0.85 dedup threshold (it almost never fires on real sentences). Alternative already applied: src/ is explicitly marked as non-building reference pseudocode in the AMM README.
objects.reason: Honesty gap identified in ecosystem review: spec claims a reference implementation but the code cannot compile or run. Low priority — the operative implementation is the reflector/curator agents + validator script.

objects.fact: Definitive infrapilot-app config map (analyst-verified with file:line): only 3 env vars are read anywhere — NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL + NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY (lib/supabase/client.ts:5-6, lib/supabase/server.ts:7-8, proxy.ts:6,15-16) and GROQ_API_KEY (app/api/cotizar/route.ts:7,14; app/api/licitaciones/analyze/route.ts:112,119, both guarded with early 500). Auth is 100% Supabase Auth: login/register call signInWithPassword/signUp client-side; full_name/company live in Auth user_metadata; every table has RLS auth.uid() = user_id. Schema is fully covered by migrations 000_initial_schema.sql (projects, budgets, activity, triggers) + 001_prices_apus.sql (price_items, apus, apu_components, budgets ALTERs) — 001 fails without 000. proxy.ts skips ALL auth checks when Supabase vars are unset (open access/mock mode).
objects.context: PEND-002 phase A analysis. Supersedes the vaguer parts of KN-004 (KN-004 remains for the mock-data behavior description).

objects.reason: Found during PEND-002 analysis. Removing is trivial; wiring Anthropic is a feature decision the user owns.
